metadata.title: Infectious Diseases SEAL/RCL Minimal Examination-Selection Extension Knowledge Base
metadata.purpose: Minimal examination-selection extension for infectious-diseases expert-agent evolution. It trains what an ID expert should examine, ask, collect, and test next from a presenting case before definitive diagnostic results.
metadata.not_for_clinical_use: Research scaffold only; not patient-specific clinical guidance, prescribing advice, local IPC policy, or public health notification guidance.
design_principles: keep diagnosis-only loop intact and add task_type == examination_selection
visible case stage is presenting case before definitive results
binary hidden judge for first smoke test
save only successful examination-selection cases and validated examination reflections
separate examination memory from diagnosis memory or store with task_type filter
emphasise specimen quality, test timing, immediate safety checks, and high-yield focused examination
avoid treatment-plan grading in this minimal extension
metadata.source_policy: Per user preference, per-condition source URLs are omitted to reduce prompt and file noise. Entries were reviewed against authoritative infectious-disease, microbiology, travel, STI, sepsis, and opportunistic-infection guidance.
review_basis_summary: CDC Yellow Book returned-traveller and travel-infection guidance
CDC STI testing and treatment guidance
IDSA skin/soft tissue, candidiasis, aspergillosis, and diabetic-foot guidance where relevant

initial_attempt: sample hidden condition or syndrome module
generate presenting case before definitive results
retrieve examination-selection successful cases and validated examination reflections
doctor answers with focused examination/history and initial tests/specimens
failure_retry: if incorrect, hidden judge returns one concise examination-selection reflection
retry the same presenting case once with reflection
if retry correct, save case and reflection
if retry incorrect, discard patient text and unvalidated reflection

judge_output_json.essential_hits: list of essential examination/test families the answer captured
judge_output_json.reflection: null if correct; otherwise one concise examination-selection lesson

memory_policy.recommended_store: separate examination_successful_cases.jsonl and examination_validated_reflections.jsonl, or unified stores filtered by task_type

selection_algorithm: within lane, prefer modules with fewer saved successes and higher task_priority
do not use prevalence as a weight
fallback to uniform examination_selection module if a lane has no available module

include enough syndrome, exposure, host-context, and severity clues to justify the expected examination/test strategy
do not reveal the disease name, pathogen name, condition ID, or definitive result
avoid adding the gold test result into the presenting vignette

usable_case_criteria: case is clinically compatible with the hidden condition or syndrome
case contains enough information to choose focused examination and tests
case does not already contain definitive diagnostic test results
case does not leak the disease or pathogen name
case does not require treatment decision-making to be answerable

core_exam_or_history: full vital signs and shock assessment
focused source exam: chest, abdomen, skin/soft tissue, lines, urinary, CNS
review devices, recent procedures, antimicrobials, immune status
essential_examination_or_tests: two sets of blood cultures from separate venipuncture sites before antimicrobials if no substantial delay
serum lactate and basic organ dysfunction labs
source-directed cultures/specimens before antibiotics when feasible
source-directed imaging when focal signs are present
conditional_or_second_line_tests: respiratory viral testing/chest imaging if respiratory source
urinalysis and urine culture if urinary symptoms or no source
line cultures if central-line source suspected
avoid_or_low_value: using procalcitonin alone to decide infection
ordering broad serology panels before cultures
delaying antimicrobials in shock to complete low-yield tests
dangerous_misses: missing occult source or source control clues

correct_if: answer includes the syndrome-defining examination or specimen/test family
answer includes immediate safety assessment when the vignette contains sepsis, airway, CNS, shock, ocular, or limb-threatening features
answer uses exposure, travel, sexual, device, immune, pregnancy, or healthcare context when central to the case
answer avoids relying only on nonspecific inflammatory markers or broad panels
incorrect_if: answer misses the essential specimen or examination that would materially narrow the diagnosis
answer proposes only treatment or disposition without examination/testing
answer delays urgent cultures/specimens where delay would impair diagnosis
answer selects a low-value or misleading test as the main next step
answer ignores host-context or exposure clues that determine the test strategy
judge.binary_scoring: 1 if answer is clinically acceptable for examination/test selection; otherwise 0
judge.reflection_target: one concise lesson about the missed examination/test strategy

core_exam_or_history: neurologic exam including mental state, focal deficits, papilledema, seizures
essential_examination_or_tests: blood cultures before antimicrobials if feasible
lumbar puncture with opening pressure, CSF cell count/differential, glucose with simultaneous serum glucose, protein, Gram stain and culture
assess whether CT head is needed before LP based on focal deficit, papilledema, seizure, immunocompromise, or marked altered mental status
conditional_or_second_line_tests: meningitis/encephalitis PCR panel when available
CSF pneumococcal/meningococcal PCR if culture likely affected by antibiotics
avoid_or_low_value: CT head for everyone before LP regardless of risk
serum inflammatory markers as the main diagnostic test
delaying antibiotics for LP/CT when unstable
dangerous_misses: missing blood cultures if LP delayed

VZV/enterovirus/arboviral PCR or serology guided by exposure and season
avoid_or_low_value: only CT head without CSF testing
routine serum HSV serology as diagnostic
waiting for MRI before CSF/empiric pathway when unsafe

core_exam_or_history: airway/respiratory distress assessment before throat manipulation
avoid forceful oropharyngeal exam in unstable airway
essential_examination_or_tests: lateral neck imaging or flexible nasolaryngoscopy only in controlled setting if stable
blood cultures if febrile/systemically ill
CBC/basic labs after airway risk addressed
conditional_or_second_line_tests: throat culture only after airway is secure and specialist-directed
CT neck if alternative deep-neck infection and stable
avoid_or_low_value: aggressive throat exam with tongue depressor in unstable patient
delaying airway evaluation for routine labs
outpatient swab-first approach
dangerous_misses: failure to assess for deep neck alternative

serology/PCR for culture-negative causes when cultures negative and suspicion persists

echocardiography alone without cultures
empiric antibiotics before cultures in stable subacute case

routine superficial skin swab without purulence
